calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Voluntary Dismissal
Off Calendar Jun-13-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- PAULO NARANJO, TEL. NO. 359-8733, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PLAINTIFF PAULO NARANJO'S Motion To Vacate Voluntary Dismissal IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS AN IMPROPER, UNTIMELY AND UNSUPPORTED MOTION TO RECONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES VIOLATES C R C 3.1113(D) AND (F). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/CK

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Voluntary Dismissal
Off Calendar Feb-28-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

PLAINTIFF PAULO NARANJO'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Voluntary Dismissal IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE PURSUANT TO AN AGREEMENT WITH THE DEFENDANT IS NEITHER A VOID JUDGMENT FOR PURPOSES OF CCP 473(D) NOR A UNILATERAL DISMISSAL THAT THE NON-DISMISSING PARTY MIGHT HAVE THE RIGHT TO CHALLENGE UNDER THE MILLER CASE. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189. (301/PJB/CK)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2006 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-13-2006 Vacated
MINI MINUTES: This matter came regularly on calendar this day, by assignment from the Department of the Presiding Judge for Settlement on the Record.
Dept. 218, JPT. Lee Harris, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Mitch Lyon.
Plaintiff: Paulo Naranjo, present in pro per.
Trevor Caudle, Esq. for Defendant: WS Young Consulting, Inc.. a California corp.
Defendant's Witness: William Young is also present in court.
Parties settled, terms read into the record as follows:
Matter settled along with case #456627 and #455657. Defendant agreed to pay the sum of $8,00.00 to Plaintiff in two installments of $4,250.00. First installment within 30 days and the next installment within 30 days after Jan. 1, 2007.
Plaintiff agreed not to appear or participate at the pending EDD Hearing and not an employee of the Defendant.
Parties waived claims against each other, and to bear own fees and costs.
Defendant will dismissed the action with prejudice today or as soon as possible.
Parties agreed to terms, matter is deemed settled, Trial vacated.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-07-2006 Text Ruling
